description: Bun does NOT honour 'process.exitCode = undefined' — it stays at the last numeric value. Tests asserting 'process.exitCode toBeUndefined()' across teardown are flaky in Bun. Pre-existing wiki.test.ts uses '= undefined' but never asserts toBeUndefined() so the leak is invisible there.
resolution: Set process.exitCode = 0 in beforeEach AND afterEach to mark 'no failure observed'; assert toBe(0) for success cases and toBe(1) for explicit failures. Never assert toBeUndefined().

name: citty-subcommand-test-access
description: Citty subcommands that aren't named exports can still be tested by reaching through the parent's subCommands map: 'await (parentCommand.subCommands as any).resolve()' returns the same singleton citty uses at runtime. Pattern: write a 'getResolveSubcommand()' helper at the top of the test file that does the await + cast. Avoids polluting src/commands/*.ts with test-only named exports just to access internal subcommands.

name: pair-accept-shared-recipients-symlink
description: When testing pair-accept collision rejection across two laptops, symlink the second laptop's recipients/ subdir to the first's. The implementation derives recipientsDir from dirname(identityPath), so distinct AM_AGE_IDENTITY_DIR values would otherwise produce isolated recipients/ dirs, and the duplicate-name guard at pair-accept.ts:131 would never trip — defeating the test.
